title_ar: شرح الحديث الثامن والثلاثين: دوافع العبادة (تابع)
content_en: He states that a person performs the obligatory deeds because he knows that they are obligatory and if he does not perform them, he may be punished. That, in itself, is sufficient to drive him to perform those deeds. As for the voluntary deeds, he knows that there is not going to be any punishment 1 Cf., Quoted by Fareed (pp. 30-3 1 ) from Muhammad ibn al-Qayyim, Tareeq al-Hijratain, p. 1 79. 2 Ibn Hajr, Fath (al-Maktaba al-Tijaariwah), vol. 13, p. 145. 1 154 Hadith #38: "Whoever Shows Enmity to . . . " awaiting him if he does not perform them. When he performs the voluntary deeds, he is doing so simply to get closer to Allah and not because they are a must upon him. Hence, he is doing them out of his love to get closer to Allah. The reward for that deed is a special love from Allah, even though the obligatory deeds receive a greater reward. Like ibn Hajr before him, al-Shaukaani also states that such a phenomenon can be witnessed among humans. Suppose a person has two slaves or servants. The first of them fulfills what the master requires of him. Therefore, the master is pleased with him because he has no shortcoming in his performance. However, the second one does what his master asks of him and, beyond that, he does other things that he knows that his master would like. The only reason he does those deeds is because he knows that they are pleasing to his master. The master then will be pleased with the second slave and he will also have a special love for him due to those extra actions that he is performing. This is the love, al-Shaukaani argues, that this hadith is referring to. It is the additional love that Allah bestows upon a person for doing deeds that Allah has not obligated of him. The slave is doing more than what is required of him simply to please Allah more. Therefore, this is a different type of love. The one who performs the obligatory deeds is beloved to Allah. There is no question about that. But the one who goes beyond that is even more beloved to Allah and gets the special love from Allah that is described herein. Hence, there are two types of love that come about due to two different cause. But, it should not be forgotten, that the first cause is a prerequisite for the second type of love. In other words, if a person does not perform the obligatory deeds, he will not receive the first or second type of love, even if he performs numerous voluntary deeds. 1 The result of this second type of love is what is described in the following portion of the hadith: Allah becomes the person's hearing by which he hears and so forth. "When I love him, I am his hearing with which he hears, his seeing with which he sees, his hand with which he strikes, and his foot with which he walks."2 The most obvious question that arises from these words of the hadith is: How does Allah become a person's hearing, seeing and so forth? This question has been answered in a number of ways. lbn Hajr presents seven different interpretations for this passage.3 There are yet others that ibn Hajr did 1 Al-Shaukaani, Qatr, pp. 419-42 1 . 2 Other versions of the same hadith state that Allah also becomes, "his heart with which he reflects and his tongue with which he speaks." 3 Ibn Hajr, Fath (al-Maktaba al-Tijaariyyah), vol. 13, pp. 146-147. 1 155 Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi not mention. These include the following (note that some of them may be overlapping): (1) It is a kind of metaphor. Its meaning is, "I become like his seeing and hearing with respect to his being influenced by My command. He loves to obey Me in a way as strong as his love for the limbs of his body." Al­ Shaukaani states that this interpretation is far-fetched. It requires a severe twisting of the meaning of the words and it also contradicts another narration which states, "And by Me he hears and by Me he sees."1 (2) The person's whole being becomes busy with what is related to Allah. Hence, he never listens to anything except what is pleasing to Allah. He never looks at anything except what is pleasing to Allah and so forth. This is the understanding of al-Khattaabi and al-Daawoodi. Al-Shaukaani states that this is better than the first interpretation. 2 (3) The person makes Allah his goal and purpose and, therefore, it is as if Allah has become the person's hearing, seeing and so forth. Al-Shaukaani argues that this interpretation makes no sense. It virtually robs the phrases of the hadith from any significance whatsoever.
content_id:
Seseorang melaksanakan amal-amal wajib karena beliau mengetahui bahwa amal-amal itu adalah wajib dan jika beliau tidak melakukannya, beliau mungkin akan dihukum. Itu sendiri sudah cukup untuk mendorongnya melaksanakan amal-amal itu. Adapun amal-amal sunnah, beliau mengetahui bahwa tidak akan ada hukuman yang menunggunya jika beliau tidak melakukannya. Ketika beliau melaksanakan amal-amal sunnah, beliau melakukannya semata-mata untuk lebih dekat kepada Allah dan bukan karena itu adalah suatu keharusan baginya. Oleh karena itu, beliau melakukannya karena cintanya untuk semakin dekat kepada Allah. Pahala untuk amal itu adalah cinta yang istimewa dari Allah, meskipun amal-amal wajib mendapatkan pahala yang lebih besar.

Al-Syaukani juga menyatakan bahwa fenomena seperti itu dapat disaksikan di antara manusia. Misalkan seseorang memiliki dua hamba atau pelayan. Yang pertama memenuhi apa yang tuannya minta darinya. Oleh karena itu, tuannya senang dengannya karena tidak ada kekurangan dalam kinerjanya. Namun yang kedua melakukan apa yang tuannya minta darinya dan, di luar itu, melakukan hal-hal lain yang diketahuinya akan disukai oleh tuannya. Satu-satunya alasan beliau melakukan amal-amal itu adalah karena beliau mengetahui bahwa amal-amal itu menyenangkan tuannya. Tuan itu kemudian akan senang dengan hamba kedua dan juga memiliki cinta yang istimewa untuknya. Inilah cinta yang dirujuk oleh hadis ini — cinta tambahan yang Allah berikan kepada seseorang karena melakukan amal-amal yang tidak Allah wajibkan kepadanya.

Ada dua jenis cinta yang timbul karena dua penyebab yang berbeda. Pertama adalah mencintai siapa yang melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban. Kedua adalah cinta yang lebih istimewa untuk siapa yang melampaui kewajiban-kewajiban dengan amal-amal sunnah. Namun perlu diingat bahwa penyebab pertama merupakan prasyarat untuk jenis cinta kedua.

'Ketika Aku mencintainya, Aku menjadi pendengarannya yang dengannya ia mendengar, penglihatannya yang dengannya ia melihat, tangannya yang dengannya ia memukul, dan kakinya yang dengannya ia berjalan.' Pertanyaan paling jelas yang muncul dari kata-kata hadis ini adalah: Bagaimana Allah menjadi pendengaran, penglihatan, dan sebagainya dari seseorang? Pertanyaan ini telah dijawab dengan berbagai cara. Ibn Hajar menyajikan tujuh penafsiran berbeda untuk bagian ini. (1) Ini adalah sejenis metafora. (2) Seluruh keberadaan orang tersebut menjadi sibuk dengan apa yang berkaitan dengan Allah. Oleh karena itu, beliau tidak pernah mendengarkan apapun kecuali yang menyenangkan Allah. Ini adalah pemahaman Al-Khatthabi dan Al-Dawudi. (3) Orang tersebut menjadikan Allah sebagai tujuan dan maksudnya sehingga seolah-olah Allah telah menjadi pendengarannya dan penglihatannya. Al-Syaukani berpendapat bahwa penafsiran ini tidak masuk akal.
content_ar: ويرى أن المرء يؤدي الفرائض لأنه يعلم أنها واجبة وأنه إن تركها قد يُعاقب، وهذا وحده كافٍ لدفعه إلى أدائها. أما النوافل فهو يعلم أنه لن يُعاقب على تركها...
